reviews.title: My experience with T-Mobile has been…
reviews.review_text: My experience with T-Mobile has been awful. The first mistake was going to a non corporate store my next mistake was believing that they have the best network in the USA. I have had nothing but problems with my signal strength and wireless network. It is the WORST performance I’ve ever had. The area I live in can not support wireless. Do yourself a favor before changing your internet provider. Google internet speed test and test your speed where you intend to have your service. Make sure you have adequate speeds. I am a disabled veteran and went to T-Mobile because of their great veteran deals. No deal here. There is no deal to be had with the aggravation they have put me through. reviews.title: We tried to join T-Mobile, what a BIG mistake that was!
reviews.review_text: We tried to join T-Mobile to move from Straight Talk/Verizon. T-Mobile sent (2) sim cards for are android phones. The sim's would not work on our less than 1 year old paid for phones because Straight Talk/Verizon had hidden locks on our phones that we purchased and refused to unlock our phones and release our phone numbers that belong to us for over 10+ years. Due to this T-Mobile "advised me" to take the sim cards to the local T-Mobile office at our local SAM'S CLUB and have the tech there cancel our account and give them the sim cards... which we did. The T-Mobile Service was "cancelled" and "never" activated because of the sims and locked phones! Then I receive a bill from T-Mobile for $70.19 for nothing except some trumped up charges they said we owed even though "the service was never activated". I sent them a letter telling them that I was not paying this unfair & fraud billing. Then about a week later I get another letter from them where they had reduced it to $29.00 with more unfair & fraud explanations. T-Mobile was basically wanting money just for "contacting" them and wanting to move our cellular service to them WHICH COULD NOT BE DONE due to Straight Talk/Verizon locking our phones. Which means we would have to purchase (2) new phones with (2) new phone numbers from T-Mobile to use their service and sim cards! BUT they billed me again for $29.00  for absolutely nothing except more fake/fraud charges. This is the way T-Mobile treats potential new customers, a high bill for nothing $70.19 then if you refuse to pay it they reduce it to a lower $29.00 bill even if you can't make their service work on your phones due to the other cellular service, in this case Straight Talk/Verizon, refusing to UNLOCK our phones and give up our phone numbers that we own! Cellular phone services have become a huge scam it seems with certain cellular network providers.

reviews.title: T-Mobile customer service & broken promises
reviews.review_text: I’ve been a T-Mobile customer for over four years and, up until recently, I would have spoken positively about my experience. However, I feel it’s important to share this so others can make informed decisions.Over two days last week, I spoke with six different representatives who all confirmed that I could switch my account to “pay only” and transfer my lines to a different carrier. I relied on that information and proceeded with the transition, only to be told later that the agreement would not be honored. When I requested to speak with a supervisor for clarification, the request was denied.I have also raised concerns for a long time about the steadily increasing cost of my bill for four phone lines and inconsistent home internet. I was previously promised a lower rate for the internet service, yet my bill continued to increase with no clear explanation.I’m not writing this out of frustration, but as a fair warning to others: transparency and follow-through are important when customers are making financial decisions. In today’s economy, families can’t afford unexpected bill increases or broken service agreements.I hope T-Mobile takes this feedback seriously and improves communication, consistency, and accountability for future customers.

reviews.title: Horrible customer service
reviews.review_text: Came in person to get an ongoing issue resolved. Should I be shocked it couldn't be resolved, no not at all. We were in Costco and AT&T switched us over to their provider company for phone service. Between phone companies they would not let us cancel our services on our own and needed to do it for us with T mobile to transfer services. We were told we don't need to do anything or take any action all services were cancelled. Well they weren't. We got new credit cards and T mobile called saying we owed money this months bill couldn't come out due to insufficient funds. We called and couldn't get through to anyone because we currently open with services from them so we could not gain access to the T-Mobile account. Months later looking into this we see they have been charging us every month since April still for services we don't have. Apparently there's an extra line that wasn't closed keep in mind we weren't able to intervene so we are thinking everything has been closed. Talking to CARE at the store over the phone they weren't helpful and blaming us saying they can't do anything so then we get transferred to someone "higher up" and said they can't do anything but credit our latest payment. She also tried to tell us everything was closed but a free line wasn't, which we never had access to or even had a phone or sim card supplied. Between carriers they failed to close the account completely when we signed documents that they were. All I wanted was credit to us since changing carriers in April and all I'm getting is rude and unhelpful reps. I asked to talk even higher up about this and they said sure it will take 72 hours to hear from anyone so keep an eye out on your phone for a call. This is very unprofessional and unacceptable that there is no one that can help with any of this and you have to patiently wait at your phone for a call from someone. We came in on a day off in person to get this resolved. I work a high stress environment healthcare job I can't be by my phone every minute waiting for a call and dropping what I'm doing. Very unhappy and will be seeking action for better customer service and credit to us for services .

Then over a yr later we had to make changes, but found out my husband wasnt the account holder, but still in my dads name and we've been paying for his line the whole time. We were told someone would call my husband to switch it over, at the same time they asked for a proof of death certificate,  which I provided.  Since they didn't call my husband,  we called but were told to call the next day. The next day all our lines were cut off.  They told us since we didn't switch the account holder by the deadline, the account was deactivated.  My husband has to have a phone for his job and since they wouldn't let us reactivate our old account with our numbers we had for over 13 yrs, we had to open a new account so he could have a phone. When I called tmobile about this,  they said the cost to activate a new account and the money paid for my dad's line would be reimbursed,  but everytime I inquire about it, nobody knows anything and tells me to go to the tmobile store with my ID and they'll help me with it. At the store they tell me there's nothing they can do and don't know why I was told that. When opening the new account, they told me I'd be able to recover our old lines, but I had to open a new account first. When we called about this they said the lines were already being given to other people.  We were just so fed up and said if we don't get our old lines, we're going to another company.  We had our lines back the next day, but never anything else on being reimbursed.  Its now been over 4 yrs

reviews.title: WARNING DON’T USE T-MOBILE
reviews.review_text: WARNING: Do Not Fall for T-Mobile’s Lies – Worst Experience ImaginableI’m writing this as a serious warning to anyone even thinking about signing up with T-Mobile. This company has mastered the art of deception, bait-and-switch tactics, and corporate gaslighting. They lure you in with flashy deals, too-good-to-be-true promotions, and promises of “unmatched customer service.” But once you’re locked in, the nightmare begins — and it only gets worse.Let me break down my experience so you know exactly what you’re getting into:1. Baited With Lies, Trapped With FeesThey roped me in with a promotional offer they had no intention of honoring. “Free phone with trade-in,” they said. “No activation fees.” “Guaranteed coverage.” Not a single one of those claims turned out to be true. When I brought it up? Suddenly the promotion “didn’t apply to my area” or “wasn’t properly applied at signup.” Their answer? Too bad — pay up or face penalties.2. Customer Service is a Joke (If You Can Even Reach Them)Calling T-Mobile support is like entering a black hole. You explain your issue five times to five different reps, none of whom can access the same notes. Promises of callbacks and escalation are just empty scripts. You’re met with fake apologies and robotic empathy, but never any actual solutions. It’s like being told “we care” while they stab you in the back with another fee.3. Dispute Resolution is a Total ScamTried to escalate my issue? Good luck. Once they’ve got your money, they hide behind “terms and conditions” you never saw. Disputes go nowhere. You're either ignored, misdirected, or gaslit into thinking you misunderstood the offer. They turn their own false advertising into your mistake.4. Product Guarantees are MeaninglessTheir so-called satisfaction guarantee is a trap. I returned a device within the window — in perfect condition — and still got hit with a “restocking fee,” late return charge, and mysteriously lost trade-in credit. You can beg, plead, provide receipts — they don’t care. Once you're in the system, you're just another monthly dollar sign.5. Deliveries and Timelines? Completely Unreliable! If they tell you “2-day shipping,” expect two weeks and a dozen excuses. “The system glitched,” “warehouse delay,” “shipping partner issue,” — they have a script for every delay. Meanwhile, your bill starts immediately, whether you have the product or not.6. Threats, Penalties, and IntimidationTried to cancel after being completely misled? They hit me with a termination fee, an equipment non-return fee (even though I returned the device), and a threat to send it to collections if I didn’t pay immediately. All because I refused to keep paying for a service they failed to provide.BOTTOM LINE:T-Mobile is a dishonest, manipulative company that uses false advertising to pull you in, delivers none of what they promise, then holds you financially hostage when you try to leave. If you value your time, money, sanity — run. There are far better carriers out there who treat their customers like people, not ATM machines. I wish someone had warned me before I made the mistake. Consider this your warning.

reviews.title: Three weeks ago
reviews.review_text: Three weeks ago, I switched from Speckroom to T-Mobile. I specifically asked not to change my phone number and transferred my existing number. I thought there might be a reason and paid it without objection. But today, I was shocked to see another $300 bill for the canceled line — a line I never asked for in the first place.The store admitted their mistake and apologized several times, but they still expect me to pay this $300 bill.In short, I believe T-Mobile is the biggest scam in America. I am currently using their service, but I absolutely do not recommend it to anyone. Believe me, in total, I have spent over 10 hours on the phone in just three weeks trying to fix their mistakes.

reviews.title: I was a loyal T-Mobile customer for…
reviews.review_text: I was a loyal T-Mobile customer for about seven years. I only cancelled because the service at my home had gotten so poor — dropped calls, barely any data signal — that it just wasn’t usable anymore.When I cancelled, I was told everything was taken care of. The phone line was turned off, and I stopped receiving any bills. Then, about three months later, I suddenly got a bill for over $300 — supposedly for a tablet and a watch line that I believed had been cancelled at the same time. The bill was actually more than what I used to pay when I had my full service active, which made no sense.There was zero usage on either device, and when I called customer service, I was basically told that it didn’t matter — I’d have to pay it or it would go to collections. I offered to mail a check for part of the amount while we sorted it out, but just a few days later, they sent the entire balance to collections anyway, even though my payment was already in the mail.After several calls, I was told “tough luck.” That’s not how a company should treat a long-time customer. I’m extremely frustrated and disappointed with how T-Mobile handled this, especially after years of loyalty.I would strongly caution anyone considering switching to T-Mobile to keep very close track of your account and confirm in writing that all lines and devices are truly cancelled. This experience has been a mess from start to finish.

reviews.title: T-Mobile's false coverage maps are just to sell you a plan - beware!
reviews.review_text: Tried T-Mobile for 3 days. Bought their highest premium mobile service plan, after making them show me on their T-Mobile website while at the retail store that my property was squarely in their highest tier of 5G+ service before my purchase, and they assured me I was. I went home and had no service for 3 days. Even when I spoke with the second tier of support service at T-Mobile, they said that there is negligible or no coverage at my property address regardless of what they promised me at the retail store. On the first day when I got home and had no service, I tried to call the store and the call dropped and I couldn't even complete one. So I drove back to the store, and they looked at some kind of screen to see if there was any trouble reported in my area and they said there were several trouble tickets made in the last few days. So I went back home and had no service all night.Next day, still no service. So I called technical support at T-Mobile. If you replay my recording of that conversation with that second tier of support, which took me quite a bit of effort to get to, you will hear her tell me when I asked her "So that's just false advertising and they lied to me at the retail store when they showed me on the map that my property is in your highest coverage area?" you will hear her say yes.Third Day still no service. So I returned to T-Mobile the brand new Pixel 10 Pro XL I had purchased and the staff still insisted on charging me a $70 restocking fee. This despite the fact that I met all return criteria, the phone was like brand, new still had the box and everything that came with it. No hardware or software alterations. According to their return policy it says they MAY,  not that they will, charge a restocking fee, so obviously it was at their discretion whether to charge one.  Do you think that they were apologetic that the service they promised me had never materialized? Or felt bad because of all the time and fuel I wasted on calls to customer support and trips back to the store I had to make in 3 days because what they promised had been a lie? It was certainly not in the interest of best business practice not to try to salvage any future relationship with me, as they went ahead and charged me $70 restocking fee anyway. I never got an apology from any of the staff, they never even ACTED sorry; rather, they treated me as if I MYSELF was the problem, despite the fact that I had been patient and cooperative even though I had had no service at my address as they promised me that I would. I will never ever buy anything from T-Mobile again. I do not recommend to anyone a store that lies as store policy, deliberately does not fact check their coverage maps so as not to miss a chance to sell someone a service that doesn't really exist, and couldn't care less about the customer and their experience. False advertising is against the law in all 50 states. I'm a 66 year old retired computer professional and I do understand a great deal about cellular data transmission and how their systems work. I guess they just assumed I was stupid. Please don't give this company your business, they won't do anything to earn it and they don't deserve it after the sale. Shame on T-Mobile. Correct your freaking maps and be truthful with your customers, and you won't create so much ill will.

reviews.title: What a headache!
reviews.review_text: What a headache!This review is solely directed to their Fiber Intent.I have had their Fiber internet service for about 10 weeks now.First issue. Scheduled installation. Day here all day waiting. Never happened. Had to call in and reschedule. When the tech arrived, he informed me that he was indeed here the previous day for installation. When I asked him if this was the case, how is it he didn't appear on any of my monitors and certainly not on my ring doorbell. Hhhmmm couldn't explain that one.Since installation, the same tech had been out twice and another tech once. My internet would go out for more than 24 hours. Strangely, each time the techs were being dispatched, my internet would suddenly come back online. Techs check the signal, "oh, look at these numbers." You have GREAT service."Explaining that this keeps happening along with the router not providing accurate information doesn't seem to matter. Their equipment showed amazing numbers and I should be happy. This morning's tech informed me that "could replace the router and cover cable, but, won't change anything." I had to ask for clarification. If I understood this statement, it translates to: this issues you are experimenting aren't for to the router Sir, it's the overall service. It's newer and still has bugs. Hhhmmm, what does that say. Now, to his credit, he would not validate my translation, he simply responded that the issue is not the router. Hearing that on top of excuses provided during the visit resulted in my asking him to leave.so, after waiting more than a day on weekdays, not weekends, to get service only to have them show up and do nothing more than show me the numbers, what's the point in even calling?I did call in after dismissing the tech just to have my concerns noted. The agent, truly amazing and skilled wanted to do more than leave it at noting the account. I should be hearing from a tech between Monday and Wednesday to get a new router. I will certainly update this with the status. If this doesn't resolve the issue, I'll be forced to cancel as they have no way to isolate where the issue is actually happening. Not like they are going to come out and start digging up cable to backtrack where the issue is.This being said, consider the following. Are you a work from home individual? If yes, this service, in its current state, could cost you your job. If you need it for security, again, in its corny state, not a good choice.UPDATEIt's now Wednesday 10/8. I was supposed to receive a call back today. Never happened. I was without service AGAIN from Saturday until around 3:30 today. More interestingly, I received a text confirming my tech support visit for Friday. Um, I didn't schedule an appointment, but, would have been nice to have them out on Monday when service was out.

So they mailed me a physical sim card to setup the second number and have both.  I received it and installed it.  Spent another 30 minutes talking to multiple different people in customer service who said they couldn't make it happen they would have to transfer me to someone else.  I give up.  Time shop for a new carrier.  This is absolutely ridiculous.

reviews.title: Customer beware:my experience at T-Mobile
reviews.review_text: On August 16th 2025I visited a T-Mobile store to purchase a new phone right away. The salesperson tried to upsell me on a promotion for a smartwatch but only if I change my plan, I decline making it clear. I only wanted a new phone and to keep my existing plan, which I've had since 1998. The salesperson then walked me through the phone options, and I selected one. He asked if I wanted to trade in my old phone and I agreed at no point, did he disclose that the trading in my phone would automatically switch me to a different plan. I only realized my plan had changed when I received my bill. It was over a $100 more than usual. I felt deceived. I never agreed to a plan change. And the lack of transparency was unacceptable. Thankfully, when I called T-Mobile, a representative named Kirk was incredibly helpful and patient. As I explained the situation lesson learned, always double-check. What any change to your account might mean? Ask direct questions and get clear answers. Don't let yourself be misled like I was.

reviews.title: Misled, Overcharged, and Deceived – Bait and switch tactics - Buyer Beware
reviews.review_text: Title: Misled, Overcharged, and Deceived – Bait and switch tactics - Buyer BewareWe were promised by a T-Mobile representative that if we switched from AT&T, we would receive free phones, promotional pricing, and an Apple employee discount.Our quoted monthly bill (before taxes) was $157. Instead, our actual bills have been $579 per month — more than $320 higher than our reliable AT&T service of 25 years.Not a single promise has been honored. We are being charged for phones that were supposed to be free, the promotional pricing never appeared, and the Apple employee discount was ignored. To make matters worse, we constantly see T-Mobile advertising these same promotions in commercials — the very ones that were used to lure us in — which makes the deception even more frustrating.We’ve had over a dozen interactions with customer service. Each time, a different representative gave us a slightly different explanation and resolution. One even changed our plan without our permission. Another representative admitted the errors were on T-Mobile’s side and removed autopay to prevent erroneous charges, but no corrections were ever made and promised follow-up calls never happened.This has been an exhausting, frustrating, and dishonest experience from start to finish. Instead of saving money, we are paying hundreds more and wasting countless hours trying to get answers.Bottom line: T-Mobile’s sales tactics feel deceptive, their commercials continue to advertise promotions that were not honored, and their customer support is inconsistent at best. We regret ever switching and warn others: do not trust their sales pitch.

After 15 years of marriage, and despite having zero history of harming or misusing accounts, I’m treated like I can’t be trusted with my own service. It feels outdated, unfair, and frankly unsafe to give one person that much power over another’s independence.All I want is the freedom to manage my own phone line — without being at the mercy of someone else’s choices. reviews.title: If I could give T-Mobile zero stars
reviews.review_text: If I could give T-Mobile zero stars, I would. This has been the single worst experience I’ve had with any service provider — internet, phone, or otherwise. It started with what seemed like a simple sign-up for their home internet service. That was my first mistake.The Internet Never WorkedFrom the moment I signed up, the internet never actually functioned. Not slow. Not spotty. Just did not work. I had to rely on a public library hotspot just to get basic online tasks done — in 2025. Absolutely ridiculous.Website Failures and Billing TrapsTheir website is laughably unreliable. I tried to set up auto-payment — it cut out in the middle of the process and didn’t save my information. No warning. No notification. Just a surprise suspension of service. When I called to resolve it, they hit me with a $70 reconnection fee and a $7.77 late fee. All this after they failed to process the payment due to their broken site.Refused to Cancel PromptlyWhen I decided I’d had enough and wanted to cancel, they told me I’d have to wait until the next billing cycle. Let me remind you: the internet never worked in the first place. It wasn’t until I threatened a chargeback that they agreed to cancel on the spot. So apparently they can cancel right away — they just choose not to unless you push hard enough.Still Waiting on a Refund — 6 Months LaterAnd here’s the kicker: six months later, I am still owed a refund. T-Mobile did not automatically process the return of my money like every other halfway-competent company on Earth. I had to call half a dozen times, each time being promised a different resolution. I even went into a physical store — guess what? They couldn’t help either. "It’s out of our hands." Of course it is.Final ThoughtsThis company is either massively understaffed, completely disorganized, or simply doesn’t care. Possibly all three. They’re quick to charge you for services they never deliver and maddeningly slow — or outright unwilling — to give you back your money when they fail. Customer service is a rotating door of empty apologies and non-answers.If you're thinking about signing up for T-Mobile's internet service, don't walk — RUN in the opposite direction. The frustration and wasted time are not worth it. I’ve never experienced a company so unprofessional and apathetic toward its customers.Oh and when I called to get help fixing the internet, they told me to use their diagnostic app — which, unbelievably, requires an internet connection to function. Let that sink in: I couldn’t fix the internet because I didn’t have internet to run the tool to fix the internet. It would be laughable if it weren’t so maddening.lol I was also asked when I cancelled if “there was anyone else in my home who wanted to take over the service”

reviews.title: Things I noticed with T-Mobile customer service
reviews.review_text: T-Mobile customer service will only be nice to customers who do surveys. As long as you have a survey history, especially if you give 10/10, they'll give you almost anything you ask for. They're more likely to compensate you with credits too. T-Mobile phone deals are a a rip-off unless it's a trade-in. If the deal requires a new line, you end up paying more than if you just get the phone at a regular price. It's only with a trade-in deal that you actually get a bit of discount. They make you believe you're saving money by getting a device for let's say as an example, 10 dollars a month. But then you have to pay for the new line which let's say costs 25 dollars plus taxes. If you add it all up and compare it with the phone's regular price, you'll see it either costs more than or is exactly the same as the regular price. The discount is just an illusion. If you're on grandfathered plans where your lines cost 10-15 bucks a month then yes, you will get a discount on the phone. But if your plan is 25 bucks plus taxes for every new line, you're better off just getting the phone regular price. And again, you need to answer surveys and give them 10/10. You'll notice they'll treat you better and will be more likely to give you credits regardless of your tenurity with the company.

reviews.title: Absolutely horrible customer service
reviews.review_text: Absolutely horrible customer service. My Iphone was stolen and I was filing a claim. I went to a store where the lady working there gave me the wrong insurance company to call - she mentioned an old one. I found the correct one online. It took over a week to get my new phone. And when I called Customer Service, the man would not help me because he kept insisting on sending  a verification code to the stolen phone. He was very short and rude, as well. He actually said, "I have given you options already", when he actually had not given me any options.Even after my new phone finally came in, the ESIM card is not activated, so I had to call Customer Service again, or reach them by the TMOBILE APP (as per what the instructions say to do). So, I couldn't call since the ESIM was not activated. I went to the website to get help. And that customer service agent verified my PII and confirmed my pin code.Although my identity was already confirmed, he still insisted on sending a verification code to the phone. The phone cannot get the code since the ESIM is not activated (and this is what I was calling for help with), and I asked if the verification  code can be emailed to me at the email on file, and he said they cannot. Basically, there is no recourse for help other than to send verification codes to devices that you cannot access, and so they refuse to help and tell you to go to the store (which was already closed).So, if you have your phone stolen, the Customer Service department is useless to help you because they are like robots that only want to send verification codes to the stolen phone. It is absolutely ridiculous. I regret paying for the replacement phone deductible of $249 now. I should have just gone with another company.

reviews.title: Terrible communication about delivery of new phone
reviews.review_text: I am a brand new T-Mobile customer who retired and then transferred all my lines from Verizon (23 year customer) to T-Mobile for cost savings.  Got a deal on the new Samsung Galaxy Fold 7 and was told at the store that I would receive it within 9 or 10 days and would receive an email confirming dates.  I was pleased because of a trip planned in 11 days and was  looking forward to having the new phone while traveling.  But when I received the email, it provided me different dates, the delivery was expected to be 12 to 18 days, not 9 or 10 as the store told me.  Following this email, there was no further communication from T-Mobile and so when 18 days passed I called looking for an update.Well this is when T-Mobile totally disappointed me.  They told me the Fold 7 was back-ordered and that they could not provide any new date on which I might actually receive it.  The most current information they had was, "IT WAS BACK-ORDERED" and they had no additional information.  When I asked them to reset my expectation if I might receive it in a day, a week, a month or a year; they told me they could not provide any possible date.Wow!  What a disappointment and such poor communication.  Surely a supervisor could provide a better response.Wow! I got more disappointment from the supervisor, who explained when new phones are released, they often become back-ordered and T-Mobile has been handling this "no communication" route the same for many years now.  What will happen once the original delivery date is missed, is nothing!  They do not send out any updated delivery date information, you must simply wait for a text message or email telling you that they have boxed up your new phone and created a tracking number and then you will get your phone in about four days after it is boxed.  But when my new phone would be boxed up was anybody's guess.  Until it's boxed, there will be no additional communication provided to me, no attempt to reset my expectations and no new target delivery date provided.  The supervisor did say when I pushed hard, that maybe I would get my phone in two weeks....maybe.So as a brand new T-Mobile customer, I am TOTALLY DISAPPOINTED with T-Mobile's approach at communicating with new customers... i.e. just ignoring them!What an amazingly poor impression T-Mobile has given me of how highly they value customers?Retired and disappointed in T-Mobile.

reviews.title: FCC Unlocking Rules Violation
reviews.review_text: I have been a loyal T-Mobile customer for over 17 years, but my recent experience has beenunacceptable and in violation of the FCC’s cell phone unlocking policy.On Thursday, I called T-Mobile to confirm my account balance and inquire about transferring myservice to another carrier, which is also a subsidiary of T-Mobile. I was told about port transfers but not informed that my device needed to be unlocked. My phone has been fully paid off since April or May 2025.After porting my number to the new carrier, I discovered I could not activate my phone because it was still locked to T-Mobile. The new carrier informed me of the lock, and I immediately contacted T-Mobile that same night. I was told an unlock request had been escalated and would be completed within 24‒72 hours.Since then, I have called multiple times, had multiple unlock requests “escalated,” and still havereceived no resolution and no communication from T-Mobile. It has now been over a week, and I ampaying for service with two carriers while being able to use my phone only on Wi-Fi.Adding to the frustration, I visited the T-Mobile store in Germantown, MD for assistance. The storemanager showed no interest in helping me, stating she “can’t help customers” with this issue andthat I should call customer service. When I explained that I could not use my phone to make the call, she refused to assist, saying she has the same customer service number I do. This dismissive and unhelpful approach is unacceptable.FCC policy requires carriers to unlock a paid-off device within two business days of receiving arequest. T-Mobile has failed to meet this standard despite multiple escalations. I am formally requesting immediate action to unlock my device and restore my ability to use the service I am paying for. If this matter is not resolved promptly, I will be filing a complaint with the FCC and considering additional actions to recover the costs I have incurred due to T-Mobile’s failure to act.I expect confirmation that my phone is unlocked within 24 hours of this letter.

T-Mobile customer service told me the monthly fee will be $135 including 2 lines and the monthly payment for the phone I purchased from them if I trade in my old iPhone. They also added a third line which I was told it’s for free and up to me to use it or not. It is not going to affect my monthly payments. Then I realized they are billing me a family plan of $170,91 which is outrages. Since then I have been calling them spending hours first to be connected then being on hold for hours. But no one wanted to solve my issue. Long story short I am thinking of changing to a different provider.

reviews.title: T is for Terrible
reviews.review_text: I became a customer on June 13th. I was very happy that day. Then I got my first bill which was $67.00 more than promised. I went to a T-mobile store where I found out a promotion had not been applied to one of the phone which accounted for 49.00 of the overcharge. I also found out I was being charged a 4th line I did not agree to. I was overcharged again today because T-mobile needs 2 or more billing cycles to fix an overcharge of their doing and I will not receive money - which is mine - but instead credit for T-mobile. Exactly how tech savvy is T-mobile that is takes upwards of 60 days to correct their error? And why do they get to decide it will be credit and not money I get to spend as I need? T is for Terrible.

reviews.title: Do not switch to T-Mobile
reviews.review_text: Do not switch to T-Mobile. They tricked me into doing so and getting a new phone “on them”. That was a lie. The phone is locked, and I am paying for it every month. Cannot pay it off to unlock it otherwise I lose the promotion they used to trick me into switching. I end up paying over $200/month and cannot use my phone as I wish.

reviews.title: T-Mobile has been okay overall
reviews.review_text: T-Mobile has been okay overall. The pricing is fair and the app is easy to use. Customer service usually helps, but sometimes it takes a while to get through. Coverage is decent in cities but drops in some rural areas. I’ve had a few dropped calls lately, which is frustrating. Still, it’s better than some carriers I’ve used before.

reviews.title: T mobile
reviews.review_text: I rarely write reviews but my experience with T-Mobile has been the worst cell phone carrier experience in my 36 years of owning a cell phone.  Every bill has overcharging,  nothing month to month is the same, customer service is so very bad,takes no less than 60 minutes to get message thru to service people and then after that comes supervisor who goes over everything and nothing gets fixed. We'll give you credit next month and next month more overcharging.  Shit company.  Boost ot metro much better

reviews.title: Can't log in to get help if phone doesn't work
reviews.review_text: Legacy plan with excellent value for my money (10 cents/minute). However, I just got a new Coolpad SNAP phone because the hinges on my other one are broken, although it still works. When I put in my SIM card and then tried to use it, I got the trash error message "No cellular network available. Connect to available Wi-Fi to make calls." The remedy shown by T-Mobile "From the Home screen, select App > Settings > General settings > Mobile network > Advanced. Select Access Point Names to check the existing APNs. Press the left soft key, then select Reset to default." did not work even though I tried multiple times.I tried to log in to T-Mobile to ask the community what to do and, upon giving my phone number and password, got "Let's confirm it's youWe sent a code by text message (SMS) to" (my phone number).HOW AM I SUPPOSED TO USE THIS WHEN I CAN'T DO ANYTHING WITH THE PHONE??? No alternative verification method is offered  so I can get around this to get help. (I could put the SIM card back in the other phone but, were I to have a problem with the other phone, I would actually be unable to access my account.)If this is how they are going to run their web site, I cannot recommend them to others.

reviews.title: Absolutely fraudulent company
reviews.review_text: Absolutely fraudulent company! How they circumvent the law is mind-blowing. Associates in stores scam you out of money. Only to be charged the money on a monthly bill.. AGAIN. Customer service will tell you there is little they can do. Other reviews are correct...they cause you to be late on your bill thereby imposing fees after fees after fees. Avoid at all costs!

Trying to activate these cards was not possible and the customer service representative was uneducated with the product and after 1 hour on the phone I was transferred to another knucklehead. This is a scam. Do not order sim cards for bringing your own phone to T-mobile. This cost me $22 and there is no way to return this as you need an account to login and check your order which was not needed when i ordered it so i don't have an account. This is a bank dispute and a class action lawsuit waiting to happen. Just like Verizon. T-Mobile is a joke

reviews.title: T-mobile has been really good to me and…
reviews.review_text: T-mobile has been really good to me and the people working at each store ive been to have been especially knowledgeable. The website works fine and its not hard to figure out at all. Thanks for getting starlink in the phones now too!

reviews.title: Dishonest and wasteful

reviews.title: T-MOBILE's misleading practices…
reviews.review_text: T-MOBILE's misleading practices guarantee that customers fall behind on their payments, enabling them to impose late fees that amount to millions of dollars a month in additional indirect profits for the company. They removed the late fee but declined to enroll me in autopay. I am an elderly senior in my 70s and lack internet access and do not have a Facebook account to post this on social media. I have been contacting T-Mobile at times with my friend's help from approximately July 2, 2025, until today, July 29, 2025, to settle the bill and enroll in autopay without incurring the additional $5.00 fee for my upcoming August 4, 2025, payment. However, they have consistently rejected my request for autopay, ensuring that I default, which will result in an extra $5.00 being added to my upcoming bill due on August 4th. All of this originated from T-Mobile disabling my ability to send and respond to text messages. Although my phone indicated that a text was sent, the messages were not actually delivered; it was a deceptive notification that led me to believe my text had been sent. By the time I realized T-Mobile's misrepresentations, three months had passed (from December to March). The total average text message count for each month was only 15 to 20 messages, but after opening a so-called ticket, T-Mobile would allow me to send texts for one day, claiming the issue was resolved, when in reality it was another scam, as they would subsequently revoke my ability to text or reply. I would receive notifications indicating sending and sent, even when nothing was actually transmitted. This situation persisted for six months, forcing me to demonstrate that I was not spamming. They had labeled me as a criminal for allegedly spamming against the law. Ultimately, I contacted the FCC, which swiftly acknowledged the problem as being associated with the T-Mobile carrier. I was astonished at how quickly the FCC representative could pinpoint T-Mobile as the mobile carrier, explaining that they had received a multitude of complaints regarding the company falsely accusing customers of spamming. On Thursday, April 3, 2025, the representative provided me with the government's definitions of spam and mass texting, along with the relevant rules and laws, assuring me that my 15-20 text messages per month did not amount to spamming, nor did the messages I had sent or received from others. They provided me an email with the following message: "We assigned your call Ticket No. 7758097. Here is a link to a consumer guide regarding your call - fcc.gov, consumers, guides,stop-unwanted-robocalls-and-texts. When I reported my discussion with T-Mobile, their technical department insisted that I disclose my text messages, as well as the names and phone numbers of those I had texted, as a condition for assisting me in resolving the issue. Once the FCC clarified that I was not engaging in any criminal activity, I felt relieved, as they had previously instilled fear in me that I had done something against the law. This fear was the reason for my calling to the FCC to assert my innocence, as I was unaware that I had violated any laws. I subsequently informed T-Mobile of their unfounded claims regarding my conduct, and only then did T-Mobile reveal that, while I was blocked from sending text messages, an unidentified individual was sending between 2000 and 3000 text messages per month using my number. When I pointed out that this occurred during the period they had restricted my texting capabilities, it became evident that they allowed others to somehow breach and access T-Mobile's database and misuse my number. The entire situation spiraled into chaos, prompting me to request adjustments; however, instead of addressing the matter thoroughly, they made absolutely no changes and to this date someone else is still texting over 2000 text messages to other using my T-Mobile phone number. The few dollars in credit they gave me represents only minor tweaks compared to the pain and suffering they had put me through. T-Mobiles arrogant attitude is as if they were doing me a favor, adjusting my account for $10 a month! This resulted in late charges, and concurrently, I lost my autopay feature. The entire ordeal has been a struggle since December 2024. This company operates as a monopoly after acquiring Sprint and several other companies like MetroPCS, thereby squeezing consumers. Furthermore, when I signed up with T-Mobile in February 2022, they assured me that I would be on a $40 plan with a lifetime price guarantee, which, as many are aware, is not the case.

reviews.title: Unwilling to take accountability for services they provide that don't work.
reviews.review_text: After being with T-Mobile for over 15 years, I would not recommend them.I got one of their promotions to open another line and get a free phone.The line I opened was for my teen. I had to also pay monthly for family mode, so I could shut off certain websites etc, to keep her protected.She was able to hack around it and get in to it.So I called and canceled her lineI was then responsible for paying for my "free phone" which, in a normal circumstance, I would totally understand. But it's not my fault she was able to hack into the monitoring system that I paid monthly for.I called and spoke to a representative as well as a supervisor and neither of them would help me out and comp my phone given the circumstances.Incredibly infuriating

reviews.title: I absolutely hate everything this…
reviews.review_text: I absolutely hate everything this company stands for. They are fraudulent scum bag conartists. They took advantage of me and my mother, this isn’t a joke. I seriously cannot put into words how much I hate this company.

reviews.title: Delighted with Damian
reviews.review_text: I'm sorry to hear about all these one star experiences. They clearly didn't go to the right store and more importantly, they did not get to work with Damian. He is the poster boy for customer service.  I just switched to T-Mobile from ATT last week and had major issues with ATT.  Damian was kind, empathetic and friendly all while actually solving my problems.  Very subtle. I was done in 20 minutes! Thanks, Damian!

reviews.title: After being over 10 years with T-Mobile…
reviews.review_text: After being over 10 years with T-Mobile and having no issue with customer service it’s been horrible the last few times I have had to troubleshoot issues and billing the past year From messing up my bill /messing up the promotions after  I reverted everything back they still manager to reinstate a sync up drive, te me the device is no longer compatible but someone still requiring me to pay for the end of the month without even being able to use it. Least you could have done is credit the unused service or cancel it on the spot not saying needs to run until the end of the monthly dates. Best way to get rid of loyal customer. Disappointment.

reviews.title: Horrific Customer Service
reviews.review_text: If you have any alternative to T-Mobile Internet service where you live, I'd encourage you to use it because their customer service is so terrible that not only do they not help, they waste a bunch of your time on the way.I ordered this morning, and I immediately went in and started setting my profile and my "free" streaming services I get with it.  The site kept asking for a T-Mobile number, so I tried their chatbot. That's useless (their AI is worse than usual, which is not ideal for a tech company). I manage to get a hold of a person to chat with, and they take some time to figure out that they can't do that, and I'll have to call in. They also can't just cancel the order via chat, even immediately after, so I call in to cancel because if I'm already having problems at this point, this is a bad idea. I call. After waiting on hold, I speak to someone who cancels it and promises me I'll get an email in a few minutes. When I haven't gotten an email after about 20, I try to check and see if it's canceled. I can't find that out without talking to a chat agent again, so I do that, and she can't tell me if it's canceled, nor can she assist me in any other way. I should just wait and let them deliver the expensive equipment to my house so I can be responsible for getting it back to them. I call again to speak to someone, who tells me they can't cancel it via telephone, so I ask for a supervisor. I'm transferred to Sales so they can help. They can't help, so I'm transferred to Care, but instead, the call drops. I have to call back. So now I'm waiting on hold, having spent hours trying to resolve this. That easy promise, no issues, just set it up? That's not true. And when you run into issues, good luck finding help.

reviews.title: Poor network coverage and hidden charges
reviews.review_text: In addition to AWFUL network coverage - TMobile has this really cool (NOT) benefit of 6 months of Apple TV -- but when you go to cancel it ... it is like going on an extended treasure hunt ... so screw 'em ...

reviews.title: I’m giving T-Mobile 0 stars for what…
reviews.review_text: I’m giving T-Mobile 0 stars for what has been the most appalling customer service experience I’ve ever had.I recently signed up for new service and ordered three iPhone 16e devices under contract #110200713297. Not only did T-Mobile completely mishandle my order—they sent the phones to an address I haven’t lived at in over five years—but they also sent the wrong phones entirely. I received two Samsung phones and an older model iPhone, despite my order form clearly stating I was to receive three iPhone 16e devices.That outdated address is not linked to my current AT&T account, not tied to the card I paid with, and not something I ever provided during this transaction. Yet, T-Mobile shipped my brand new devices there without verifying anything.When I called customer service, I spoke with a supervisor named Mark (Operator ID: 625715) who admitted it was their fault, but told me my options were:•	Wait for the phones to be misdelivered and hope they were returned,•	Or pay $14.99 out of my own pocket to reroute the package through UPS—money I shouldn’t have to spend to fix their mistake.Then the worst happened: on July 26 at 1:55 PM, someone impersonating me signed for the package at the incorrect address. I filed a police report immediately (#T25009110) and contacted T-Mobile again. I was told by Dem (Operator ID: 732602) that I would have to wait until Monday for someone to follow up. Apparently, theft and fraud aren’t urgent enough to warrant weekend support.Let me recap what T-Mobile got wrong:•	Sent the wrong devices•	To the wrong address•	Allowed a fraudulent signature to accept them•	Refused to take action until the next business day•	Told me I would need to repay the $117 in taxes if I wanted to start a new order in personI’ve also reached out to UPS, the Orange County Sheriff’s Office, and Fox 35 News because this situation has gone beyond poor service—this is gross negligence and a complete lack of accountability.If this is how T-Mobile treats new customers, I can only imagine how they treat loyal ones. If you value your personal information, your money, or basic customer support—do not trust T-Mobile.

reviews.title: Customer support failure
reviews.review_text: 7 years with TMobile, only to face this disgraceful scam. Your support is absolutely irresponsible, with no access to managers. Just agents endlessly bouncing me around, making me fill out the same form repeatedly. Two days wasted, zero solution. Unacceptable.

reviews.title: T-Mobile Needs Better Crisis Support
reviews.review_text: I’m currently dealing with a serious personal emergency and urgently need support from T-Mobile. In a time like this, I expected better access to real help and flexibility—especially from a major company that claims to care about its customers.I’ve tried to get in touch with someone who understands the urgency of my situation, but I haven’t been able to reach a representative who can help me arrange a temporary solution. There needs to be an emergency service option for customers in crisis—life doesn’t always wait for billing cycles.This experience has left me feeling abandoned by a provider I’ve trusted. I hope T-Mobile sees this and makes changes to ensure real, compassionate support is available when people truly need it.

reviews.title: BAD TMOBILE CUSTOMER SERVICE
reviews.review_text: Nearly five years ago, I separated from a shared plan I had with a friend. I completed a Change of Responsibility in-store with a T-Mobile representative, where I removed myself as the primary account holder and created a new account for myself and my daughter. I was assured at the time that I would no longer be financially or contractually tied to the old account. That should have been the end of it. Spoiler alert: it wasn’t.Fast forward to April 2025—I went into a T-Mobile store to upgrade my phone after it stopped charging. Instead of a quick, simple process, I spent four hours in the store being told that I was still listed on the old account—the one I had supposedly been removed from years ago. Because that account had a balance (which had absolutely nothing to do with me), I was told I couldn’t upgrade my phone unless I paid it. Let that sink in: I was being held financially responsible for a line I didn’t use, on an account I was told I was no longer part of.A representative in the store had to call customer service multiple times just to push through the upgrade. She apologized, acknowledged the repeated mistakes, and told me once again that I was finally removed from the old account. As a gesture, she provided a free screen protector and phone case. At that point, I naïvely thought this nightmare was finally over.Then came July 2025, when my daughter tried to upgrade her phone—only to be told she couldn’t because I was still listed on that same delinquent account. The same one we supposedly corrected. Again. I was never notified, never consulted, and never gave permission to be re-added to that account. But because of T-Mobile’s mishandling of my information, I was told I had to pay $468 just so my daughter could upgrade her phone on our own plan.This isn’t just a clerical error—it’s systemic negligence. T-Mobile repeatedly gave me false information, re-attached me to an account I had no involvement with, and made me financially responsible for charges I did not incur. They failed to notify me at any point about being added back onto the account, and there were no active lines even associated with it.T-Mobile has shown a clear pattern: they prioritize collecting money over providing honest, competent service. I’ve spent time, money, and peace of mind dealing with an issue that should have been resolved years ago—only for it to be mishandled over and over again.Customers deserve transparency and accountability. T-Mobile has given me neither.To make matters worse, when I called customer service in sheer frustration, I escalated the issue to a supervisor. After reviewing the situation, the supervisor personally guaranteed that the $468 I was forced to pay would be the final resolution—that this was the last time I would have to deal with any issues related to that old account. She assured me, in no uncertain terms, that I was permanently removed and would not be contacted or held responsible for anything further. Yet given T-Mobile’s track record so far, I have little faith that this promise will be honored. If history is any indication, I’ll likely be dealing with this same issue again in a few months—because that’s how little control and oversight this company seems to have over its own systems.
